sourceTitle: How to use the SUM function in Microsoft Excel
originalDuration: 38s (1280x720, vp9, re-downloaded 2026-07-13 via brew yt-dlp after the pinned ~/.local/bin/yt-dlp shim's anaconda python interpreter was found missing)
interaction: Select a cell, type =SUM(, select a range of cells to add (H5:H8), type ) and press Enter, the formula commits and the summed result appears in the cell (43) with a 'Total' callout. Same kind of interaction as this family's demo story (select a cell, type =SUM(C5:C7), result + status-bar sum reveal) -- differs only in the exact range/cells picked.
note: Vendor tutorial overlay (Windows Excel ribbon chrome; ribbon icons and title bar are directional only, not Mac Excel) but the grid/formula-bar mechanics are real current Excel UI and the SAME story this family's demo tells: select cell -> type =SUM( -> select range -> close paren + Enter -> result reveal. Frames (fps=2, 41 frames) show: function-tooltip appearing under the cell as soon as '=SUM(' is typed, the marching-ants/dashed selection outline while picking the range, and the commit-on-Enter reveal of the summed value. No login, no bot-check involved (public YouTube video, no auth). Chosen because no Excel is installed on this machine (checked /Applications, mdfind -- empty) and no office.com session was reachable/permitted (family tier is online-only and Part 1.5 hard rules forbid logging in), so this is the highest rung actually available per docs/reference-and-asset-sourcing.md Part 1.5 (rung 3: official video frame-mining).
